title: Karmaflow: The Rock Opera Videogame
notes: Doesn't start, needs .Net 4.0

title: The Turing Test
notes: no issues so far, played only 10 minutes

title: The Turing Test
notes: Amazing performance. Xbox One Controller works.

title: The Turing Test
notes: Initially the unreal directx setup complains about an invalid ACL. The game then launches. Causes a nag at the beginning of each launch. Does not impact game play.
Plays perfectly but audio is very broken (known 64-bit Wine issue)
title: The Turing Test

notes: Playable, some volumes are messed up and unchangable, voices and dialogs are not audible.
title: The Turing Test

gpu: X.Org AMD Radeon (TM) RX 480 Graphics (POLARIS10, DRM 3.26.0, 4.18.11-arch1-1-ARCH, LLVM 8.0.0)
notes: The games requires input to install some .net stuff, but shows an ACL error, but the game will start. First I had to kill the process several time and restart the game, then it worked quite ok. Audio is crackling.
title: The Turing Test

notes: Audio Cracks. With audio turned off, enjoyable puzzle experience.
title: The Turing Test

notes: The game has serious audio issues and lots of noise. The performance is a little slower than windows. It only works in DX11 mode, in DX12  mode the game doesn't start
title: The Turing Test

notes: dx 11 performance is good, however there is a steady static noise in the background while playing the game. UE Prerequesites will install with an ACL error, but game launches anyway. dx12 version did not start
title: The Turing Test

notes:
performance is good, dx11 runs fine even though the UE prerequesites installer will complain about an invalid ACL every launch. dx12 won't launch. initially i had audio issues, which i fixed by doing:
"I manually added "xaudio2_7"="native,builtin" to the end of the [Software\Wine\DllOverrides] section of Steam/steamapps/compatdata/499520/pfx/user.reg"
i found this information at https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/263, there are a couple other proposed tweaks there, the one above was enough in my case
title: The Turing Test

notes: Played till Section B. Runs fine. The only problem was the broken audio. I had to add "xaudio2_7"="native,builtin" to the [Software\Wine\DllOverrides] section of Steam/steamapps/compatdata/499520/pfx/user.reg to fix broken audio.
title: The Turing Test

notes: After the latest FAUDIO update on proton 3.16-5  all the audio glitches are gone. This game is now perfect playable on proton.
title: The Turing Test

notes: the 3.16-5 updates to the audio system have fixed any audio issues, runs perfectly now
title: The Turing Test

notes:
Me helped for audio only trick with below "Launch Options":

PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC=120 PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1

Nothing less, nothing more. Proton 3-16-6-Beta. Messing with registry either manually or by "winecfg" always was causing that game either no started or started with cracking sound. Maybe because of Polish locales? I don't know, never mind...

I was playing with game soundtrack played by Spotify - now I will have to start game from beginning, because there is more fun! Thanks guys :)
title: The Turing Test

notes: UE installer complains about ACL on every launch, but the game itself runs almost perfectly. Good performance, no audio issues. Some semi-transparent materials aren't rendered while examining objects, but this doesn't affect gameplay at all. DX12 version doesn't start.
title: The Turing Test
